(prep.) . of; some, some of, (a) part of; belonging to, pertaining to, from among ; من‎ كان‎ to belong or pertain to, be among …, fall under ...; ان‎ ذلك‎ من‎ (anna) among other things ...; من‎ منھم‎ (man) some of them; من‎ ومنھم‎ – من‎ منھم‎ some of them others ..., there are (were) those who and others who ...; قريش‎ من‎ رجل‎ (rajul) a man of the Quraysh tribe; الايام‎ من‎ يوم‎ (yaum, ayyām) some day, some day or other; الامور‎ من‎ أمر‎ (amr) s. th. or other, some affair, some business; الطعام‎ من‎ اكل‎ (ṭa‘ām) he ate (a little, some) of the food; الكتب‎ من‎ رايته‎ ما‎ mā ra’aituhū min al- kutub (what I have seen of the books) = the books I have seen; كتب‎ من‎ رايته‎ ما‎ (what I have seen of books) = what(ever) books I have seen; شريك‎ من‎ لله‎ ما‎ God has no partner whatsoever; يقدر‎ احد‎ من‎ ما‎ (aḥadin yaqdiru) nobody can ...; شخص‎ من‎ ما‎ (šaḵṣin) there ia absolutely none who ... consisting of, made of, of (material) ;حرير‎ من‎ ثوب‎ (ṯaub) a garment of silk, a silk dressat, on (time) ; الليل‎ من‎ (lail) at night; من‎ يومه‎ (yaumihī) on the very same day; من‎ ساعته‎ at that moment, at once, right away at, on, by (place) ; منكبه‎ من‎ ھزه‎ (hazzahū, mankibihī) he shook him by the shoulderlike, as, such as, as for instance; namely, to wit ; والارادة‎ والقدرة‎ العلم‎ من‎ ازلية‎ صفات‎ (ṣifāt azalīya, ‘ilm, qudra, irāda)eternal attributes such as knowledge, power, volition; ان‎ من‎ الجرائد‎ قالته‎ ما‎ صح‎ اذا‎ (ṣaḥḥa) if what the newspapers say is true, namely that ... in an exclamation:ليلة‎ من‎ طولھا‎ يا‎ yā ṭūlahā min lailatin oh, the length of the night!
